<!-- walkthrough_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

모노레포로의 이전과 확장 가능한 프로바이더 아키텍처를 도입했습니다. 비동기 파이프라인(VertexForager), 흐름 제어(FlowController), HTTP 실행기(HttpExecutor), 스키마/레지스트리, DuckDB 및 메모리 작성자, Sharadar 라우터·클라이언트, CLI/팩토리/유틸리티 및 광범위한 테스트를 추가했습니다.

## Changes

|Cohort / File(s)|Summary|
|---|---|
|**설정 및 패키지** <br> `\.coderabbit\.yaml`, `\.gitignore`, `pyproject\.toml`, `packages/vertex-forager/pyproject\.toml`|리포지토리 검사/무시 규칙 추가 및 패키지 의존성, pytest 옵션, workspace 선언 등 빌드/개발 설정 업데이트.|
|**공용 진입점 & CLI** <br> `packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/api.py`, `packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/cli.py`|공용 심볼 재내보내기와 Click 기반 CLI(collect/status/clear) 추가.|
|**클라이언트 팩토리 / 베이스클래스** <br> `packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/clients/__init__.py`, `.../clients/base.py`|프로바이더 등록/생성(create_client) 로직과 비동기 BaseClient(세션, 컨트롤러, _run 등) 구현.|
|**핵심 모델·레지스트리·재시도** <br> `.../core/config.py`, `.../core/registries.py`, `.../core/retry.py`|EngineConfig/FetchJob/FramePacket/RunResult 등 타입 모델, 제네릭 Registry, Tenacity 기반 재시도 컨트롤러 추가.|
|**흐름 제어 및 HTTP 레이어** <br> `.../core/controller.py`, `.../core/http.py`|GradientConcurrencyLimiter + GCRA 기반 FlowController와 HttpExecutor 및 default_async_client 구현.|
|**파이프라인 엔진** <br> `.../core/pipeline.py`|우선순위 큐 기반 producer–fetcher–writer 비동기 파이프라인(VertexForager) 구현(병렬 fetch/parse/normalize, bulk write, 오류 집계, 페이징).|
|**스키마·매퍼·레지스트리** <br> `.../schema/config.py`, `.../schema/mapper.py`, `.../schema/registry.py`, `.../schema/__init__.py`|TableSchema 데이터클래스, SchemaMapper(캐스트·열정렬·날짜표준화), 중앙 스키마 레지스트리 및 재내보내기 추가.|
|**Writer 인터페이스 및 구현** <br> `.../writers/base.py`, `.../writers/memory.py`, `.../writers/duckdb.py`, `.../writers/__init__.py`|BaseWriter 추상화, InMemoryBufferWriter, DuckDBWriter(동시성 락·스키마 동기화·upsert), writer 팩토리(create_writer) 추가.|
|**Sharadar 공급자 전체 경로** <br> `.../providers/sharadar/schema.py`, `.../providers/sharadar/utils.py`, `.../providers/sharadar/router.py`, `.../providers/sharadar/client.py`, `.../providers/sharadar/__init__.py`|테이블 스키마 상수, OptimizedBulkCalculator, SharadarRouter(작업 생성·페이로드 파싱), SharadarClient(여러 공개 fetch 메서드), lazy import/노출 추가.|
|**라우터 등록/팩토리 및 베이스** <br> `packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/routers/__init__.py`, `.../routers/base.py`|Sharadar 등록과 create_router 팩토리, BaseRouter 추상 인터페이스 추가.|
|**유틸리티·헬퍼·초기화** <br> `packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/utils.py`, `packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/core/__init__.py`|심볼 처리, 메모리 안전 체크, Spinner, app root/cache 관리, jupyter-safe 데코레이터 및 core 재내보내기 추가.|
|**테스트·검증 스위트** <br> `packages/vertex-forager/tests/...`|단위·통합 테스트 대거 추가(HTTP executor, DuckDBWriter, Router, Client, Pipeline, 팩토리 등) 및 pytest fixtures/검증 스크립트 추가.|

## Sequence Diagram(s)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Client as BaseClient
    participant Pipeline as VertexForager
    participant Controller as FlowController
    participant Router as BaseRouter
    participant HTTP as HttpExecutor
    participant Mapper as SchemaMapper
    participant Writer as BaseWriter

    User->>Client: _run(router, dataset, symbols, writer, mapper)
    Client->>Pipeline: VertexForager.run(dataset, symbols)
    Pipeline->>Router: generate_jobs(...)
    Router-->>Pipeline: FetchJob
    loop for each job
      Pipeline->>Controller: throttle() (rate + concurrency)
      Controller-->>Pipeline: permit
      Pipeline->>HTTP: fetch(RequestSpec)
      HTTP-->>Pipeline: payload bytes
      Pipeline->>Router: parse(job, payload)
      Router-->>Pipeline: ParseResult(packets, next_jobs)
      Pipeline->>Mapper: normalize(packet)
      Mapper-->>Pipeline: FramePacket
      Pipeline->>Writer: write_bulk(packets)
      Writer-->>Pipeline: WriteResult[]
    end
    Pipeline-->>Client: RunResult
    Client-->>User: RunResult
```

## Estimated code review effort

🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~60 minutes

## Poem

> 🐰 토끼가 말하노니:
> 모노레포에 새 길을 낸 뒤,
> 비동기 숲에 파이프라인이 춤추네.
> DuckDB에 씨앗 심고 레지스트리로 묶어,
> 테스트와 타입이 수확을 지켜주리. 🥕✨

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

**Actionable comments posted: 46**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents</summary>

```
In @.coderabbit.yaml:
- Around line 5-22: The ignore_paths key is placed under auto_review but must be
moved to reviews.path_filters; remove ignore_paths from under auto_review and
add equivalent exclude patterns under reviews.path_filters using the '!' prefix
(e.g., '!**/uv.lock', '!**/__pycache__/**', etc.) so the file filtering follows
the CodeRabbit schema and exclusion semantics; update or remove the original
ignore_paths block and ensure reviews.path_filters contains the same patterns
with '!' prefixes.
- Around line 53-54: 설정에서 유효하지 않은 속성 chat.enabled 를 제거하거나 올바른 속성으로 바꾸세요:
CodeRabbit 스키마는 chat 섹션에 art, auto_reply, integrations 만 허용하므로 chat.enabled 항목을
삭제하거나 필요한 동작에 맞춰 chat.art, chat.auto_reply 또는 chat.integrations 중 적절한 키로 대체해 주세요
(기존 chat.enabled 참조를 사용하는 코드가 있다면 해당 참조도 함께 업데이트).

In `@packages/vertex-forager/pyproject.toml`:
- Around line 19-20: Move the test packages "pytest" and "pytest-asyncio" out of
the main dependencies list and add them under the dev dependency group: remove
the entries "pytest>=9.0.2" and "pytest-asyncio>=1.3.0" from the primary
dependency array and instead declare them under the [dependency-groups].dev (or
equivalent dev dependencies) section so they are only installed for
development/testing; ensure the version specifiers remain unchanged and that any
tooling reading pyproject.toml (e.g., pip, poetry) will recognize the new dev
group.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/cli.py`:
- Around line 10-21: The CLI command function collect currently contains a TODO
instead of calling the Collector Core; replace the placeholder with a clear
invocation to your collection layer (e.g., call CollectorCore.collect or
CollectorClient.collect_symbols) that iterates over the provided symbol tuple,
passes the selected source, and returns/streams progress; add basic error
handling and user feedback (success/failure per symbol) and ensure the function
imports and uses the collector entrypoint (look for symbols collect,
main.command, and the CollectorCore/CollectorClient class or function in your
repo) — if the Collector Core is not yet implemented, create a minimal skeleton
function (CollectorCore.collect_symbols or similar) and/or open an issue linking
this CLI to that implementation.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/clients/base.py`:
- Around line 18-45: BaseClient inherits from ABC but defines no abstract
methods; declare the required interface by adding one or more
`@abstractmethod-decorated` signatures (e.g., async def get_price_data(self, ...):
and any other provider-required methods referenced in the docstring) so
subclasses are forced to implement them. Import and use abc.abstractmethod,
place the abstract method stubs inside the BaseClient class (matching expected
names like get_price_data, any pipeline entrypoints such as run or fetch, and
context-manager hooks if needed) and keep bodies as pass or raise
NotImplementedError to document the contract.
- Around line 144-159: The _http_client asynccontextmanager currently annotates
its return as httpx.AsyncClient but must be declared as an AsyncGenerator to
match `@asynccontextmanager` semantics; update the signature of _http_client to
return typing.AsyncGenerator[httpx.AsyncClient, None] (and add/import
AsyncGenerator from typing or typing_extensions as needed) and ensure any
related type hints or mypy stubs referencing _http_client use the new
AsyncGenerator form; no behavior changes to default_async_client, self._client
handling, or the yield/close logic are required—only the return-type annotation
and import.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/core/__init__.py`:
- Around line 1-10: Docstring lists a `retry` module but the package does not
import or export it; update the package to make doc and exports consistent by
either removing `retry: Retry strategies` from the module docstring or by
importing and re-exporting the retry module from this package (add `from .
import retry` and include "retry" in the module's __all__ alongside "pipeline",
"config", "controller", "http" so consumers can access retry via the package).
Ensure you edit the top-level __init__.py symbols (the docstring and the __all__
list) and the package imports (e.g., the existing imports for
pipeline/config/controller/http) to reflect the chosen change.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/core/controller.py`:
- Around line 59-65: The release() method updates state and notifies outside the
condition lock causing missed notifications; move the state mutation and notify
into the same lock: wrap self._update_limit(rtt) and self.inflight -= 1 inside
async with self._condition and then call self._condition.notify() while holding
the lock (i.e., ensure in release() the calls to _update_limit, decrement of
inflight, and _condition.notify() all occur within the async with
self._condition block).

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/core/pipeline.py`:
- Around line 293-297: The current use of lambda in loop.run_in_executor:
lambda: self._router.parse(job=job, payload=payload) can suffer from closure
binding issues; replace it with an explicitly bound callable such as
functools.partial(self._router.parse, job=job, payload=payload) or a lambda that
binds defaults (lambda job=job, payload=payload: self._router.parse(job=job,
payload=payload)) so parse_result is computed with the correct job and payload
when passed to loop.run_in_executor.
- Around line 301-308: The lambda used in loop.run_in_executor closes over the
loop variable 'packet' (from parse_result.packets) causing a late-binding bug;
replace the lambda with a non-capturing callable such as
functools.partial(self._mapper.normalize, packet) or an inner function that
binds packet as a default argument so that loop.run_in_executor(None, ...)
receives the correct packet instance before awaiting and then continue to await
pkt_q.put(normalized_packet) as before.
- Around line 73-80: Move the import out of __init__ to the module level to
avoid per-instance import overhead: add "from typing import TYPE_CHECKING" at
top and under "if TYPE_CHECKING: from vertex_forager.writers.memory import
InMemoryBufferWriter" to keep type hints without runtime import, then at runtime
do a guarded import like "try: from vertex_forager.writers.memory import
InMemoryBufferWriter except Exception: InMemoryBufferWriter = None" (or set to
None) and update the existing isinstance check in __init__ (the block that sets
self._flush_threshold and references config.flush_threshold_rows) to first
ensure InMemoryBufferWriter is not None before calling isinstance; keep the same
logic for setting self._flush_threshold.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/core/registries.py`:
- Around line 35-48: The docstring for register says it raises ValueError on
duplicate keys but the implementation silently overwrites; update register to
match the docstring by raising a ValueError when key already exists (use the
method name register and the internal dict _registry in your check) and include
a clear error message (e.g., "key already registered: {key}"); alternatively, if
you prefer overwrite-on-reload behavior, change the docstring to state that
duplicates are allowed and the item will be replaced, mentioning the reload
scenario—pick one consistent behavior and apply it to the register method and
its docstring.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/core/retry.py`:
- Line 24: The default retry_on=(Exception,) is too broad; update the retry
configuration so retry_on is narrowed to recoverable exceptions (e.g., use
retry_on=(ConnectionError, TimeoutError) or other network/IO-related exception
types) or change the default to None and require callers to pass explicit
retry_on values; locate the retry_on parameter definition in retry.py (the
declaration currently shown as retry_on: tuple[type[Exception], ...] =
(Exception,)) and modify its default and update any docstring/usage to reflect
the new default behavior.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/providers/sharadar/__init__.py`:
- Around line 19-28: The __getattr__ function lacks a return type hint which
prevents type checkers from understanding the lazily-imported symbols; update
def __getattr__(name: str) to include an appropriate annotation (e.g., -> Any)
and import Any from typing, or for stricter typing use a Union/Type alias
referencing SharadarClient and SharadarRouter guarded by TYPE_CHECKING and
string forward refs; ensure references to SharadarClient and SharadarRouter (the
lazy imports) are used only at runtime while annotations let static checkers
infer the returned symbol types.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/providers/sharadar/client.py`:
- Around line 116-136: When _fetch_by_tickers fails inside
_create_optimized_bulk leaving meta_df None, add a short-lived failure
cache/flag to avoid repeated fetch attempts: introduce a timestamped attribute
(e.g., self._last_meta_fetch_failure) checked at the top of
_create_optimized_bulk before calling self._fetch_by_tickers and skip the fetch
if the failure is recent (returning directly to the heuristic
self._optimizer.optimize() path); on a failed fetch set/update the timestamp and
on a successful fetch clear it so future calls can retry. Ensure references to
_fetch_by_tickers, _create_optimized_bulk, meta_df, and
self._optimizer.optimize() are preserved so maintainers can locate and verify
the change.
- Around line 206-290: _fetch_by_tickers repeats the manual writer
context-management pattern from _fetch_pagination (calling await
writer.__aenter__() and __aexit__() plus Spinner logic), which duplicates code
and risks improper cleanup on exceptions; refactor to use an async context
manager or a small helper to manage create_writer(...) and the Spinner so you
can simply "async with create_writer(connect_db) as writer" (or call a shared
async helper used by both _fetch_by_tickers and _fetch_pagination) and move the
Spinner/finalize logic inside that helper so exceptions always trigger proper
__aexit__() — update _fetch_by_tickers to remove direct __aenter__/__aexit__
calls and rely on the shared context handling while keeping the same use of
router, _run, create_pbar_updater, and writer.collect_table(table_name).
- Around line 157-204: The code manually calls await writer.__aenter__() and
await writer.__aexit__(None, None, None) which loses exception information and
is not exception-safe; change to use an async context manager or forward real
exception info into __aexit__. Replace the manual enter/exit around the
router/_run call and Spinner handling by either using "async with writer:" (via
create_writer) so __aexit__ receives exception details, or if you must keep
manual control to show Spinner during finalization, capture the current
exception with sys.exc_info() in the finally block and call await
writer.__aexit__(exc_type, exc_value, exc_tb); also consider wrapping writer
exit in a small custom async context manager that displays Spinner while calling
writer.__aexit__ to keep UX behavior (referencing create_writer,
writer.__aenter__/writer.__aexit__, Spinner, and the _run invocation).

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/providers/sharadar/router.py`:
- Around line 48-68: The three identical constants _TICKERS_PAGINATION_CONTEXT,
_SP500_PAGINATION_CONTEXT, and _GENERIC_PAGINATION_CONTEXT should be collapsed
into a single shared constant (e.g., _PAGINATION_CONTEXT) and the duplicate
definitions removed; replace all uses of _TICKERS_PAGINATION_CONTEXT,
_SP500_PAGINATION_CONTEXT, and _GENERIC_PAGINATION_CONTEXT in this module with
the new _PAGINATION_CONTEXT (or another chosen name) so behavior is unchanged,
keeping the same "pagination" dict structure and values.
- Around line 179-181: The current extraction
json_df.select(pl.col("meta")).item(0) can raise IndexError when the DataFrame
is empty or the meta cell is null; modify the meta extraction in the router to
first verify json_df has rows (e.g., check json_df.height or json_df.shape[0])
and that the selected value is not None before calling .item(0), or wrap the
access in a safe try/except that assigns meta = {} on any
IndexError/None/ValueError; update the code around
json_df.select(pl.col("meta")).item(0) and the meta variable assignment to use
this safe check/fallback.
- Around line 128-131: The per_page conversion using
int(str(kwargs.get("per_page", 10000))) can raise ValueError for non-numeric or
empty inputs; update the router logic around the dataset check so that you
safely coerce kwargs.get("per_page") into a validated integer (e.g., try/except
or a helper that returns the default 10000 on failure), enforce sensible bounds
(>=1 and an upper limit if needed), and then call yield
self._build_pagination_job(dataset=dataset, per_page=per_page) with the
validated value; target the block containing kwargs.get("per_page", 10000) and
_build_pagination_job.
- Around line 211-216: The broad catch in the Polars parsing block (the except
Exception: that falls back to self._decode_payload and self._datatable_to_frame)
masks real errors; narrow it to specific parse-related exceptions (e.g., Polars
JSON/IO errors, ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError) or re-raise after logging, and
add a warning log with exc_info when falling back so the original failure is
visible; update the except clause around the Polars parsing in router.py (the
block handling payload and job.dataset) to only handle parsing exceptions and
ensure the original exception is logged before using the fallback.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/providers/sharadar/schema.py`:
- Line 223: The unique_key tuple on the SHARADAR_SF2 schema incorrectly
references a non-existent "date" column; update the unique_key in
packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/providers/sharadar/schema.py (the
unique_key definition for SHARADAR_SF2) to use one of the actual date columns
defined in that schema (e.g., "filingdate" or "transactiondate" depending on
business logic), replacing "date" with the chosen column so the tuple only
contains valid column names ("provider", "ticker", "<chosen_date_column>",
"rownum").
- Line 241: The unique_key tuple for the SHARADAR_SF3 schema incorrectly
references a non-existent column "date"; update the unique_key definition in
packages/vertex_forager/src/vertex_forager/providers/sharadar/schema.py (the
unique_key tuple) to use the existing "calendardate" column instead of "date" so
the tuple becomes ("provider", "ticker", "calendardate", "investorname",
"securitytype"), ensuring it matches the SHARADAR_SF3 schema.
- Line 188: The unique_key tuple in the SHARADAR_SF1 schema incorrectly includes
a non-existent column "date"; update the unique_key definition (the tuple named
unique_key in the SHARADAR_SF1 schema) to use the correct date column—replace
"date" with "calendardate" (or remove "date" and use the appropriate date column
after confirming with Sharadar docs) so deduplication won't raise KeyError.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/providers/sharadar/utils.py`:
- Around line 116-154: The datetime.strptime calls used when building meta_map
from metadata.iter_rows (parsing s and e using start_col/end_col) and when
parsing start_date/end_date to req_start/req_end should be wrapped in try-except
blocks that catch ValueError: for the metadata loop, if parsing s or e fails,
log a debug/warn and skip that row (do not insert into meta_map); for
start_date/end_date parsing, catch ValueError and fall back to req_start=None
and req_end=date.today() (or similar existing default) and log a warning. Locate
the parsing sites referenced by the variables/functions metadata.iter_rows,
start_col, end_col, meta_map, start_date, end_date, req_start, and req_end and
add minimal try/except handling around datetime.strptime to prevent ValueError
from aborting optimize().

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/routers/__init__.py`:
- Line 5: Remove the unused logging import and the unused logger variable to
clean up dead code: locate the top-level import of logging and the creation of
logger (the symbol names "logging" and "logger") in the module and delete both,
ensuring no other code in the module references logger; run tests or lint to
confirm no remaining usages.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/schema/config.py`:
- Around line 10-21: The docstring's Attributes section is missing a description
for the analysis_date_col field; update the class/module docstring to include
analysis_date_col with its type (str | None), meaning (optional column name used
as the analysis/timestamp column for time-based processing), and default (None)
similar to the existing entries for table, schema, and unique_key so readers
understand its purpose and default behavior; reference the analysis_date_col
symbol and add this line to the Attributes list near the other fields.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/schema/mapper.py`:
- Around line 112-140: The _normalize_date_column logic currently renames an
existing "date" to "date_original" even when the specified target_col is missing
from the frame, which removes the valid "date" column; fix this by only
performing collision handling and renaming when target_col is present in
frame.columns (i.e., check `if target_col in frame.columns` before touching
"date"), then rename the existing "date" to "date_original" only when both
target_col != "date" and target_col exists, and finally rename target_col to
"date" using frame.rename; reference symbols: _normalize_date_column,
target_col, frame.columns, frame.rename, "date_original".

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/utils.py`:
- Around line 132-260: The spinner currently emits ANSI escape sequences
unconditionally which pollutes logs in non-TTY environments; detect TTY and skip
ANSI controls and animated output when sys.stderr.isatty() is False. Add an
instance flag (e.g., self._is_tty = sys.stderr.isatty()) in Spinner.__init__,
then guard emitting ANSI and cursor hide/show in _hide_cursor, _show_cursor,
_clear_line, and the _spinner_task (and in start/stop) so that when _is_tty is
False you either write plain messages once (no escape codes, no spinner loop) or
simply no-op the ANSI calls and avoid background thread creation; keep logging
buffer behavior unchanged.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/writers/__init__.py`:
- Around line 12-63: The create_writer function misinterprets Windows absolute
paths as URI schemes because it calls urlparse on every string; change
create_writer to only treat string inputs as URIs when the input contains '://',
e.g. if isinstance(connect_db, str) and '://' in connect_db: parsed =
urlparse(connect_db) ...; keep the existing writer_registry lookup flow inside
that branch to return factory(connect_db) for registered schemes; additionally
ensure duckdb URIs produce a proper filesystem path by combining parsed.netloc
and parsed.path when constructing a DuckDBWriter (or ensure the duckdb factory
_duckdb_factory receives the full uri so its replace("duckdb://", "") yields the
correct Path). Use the symbols create_writer, urlparse, writer_registry,
_duckdb_factory, and DuckDBWriter to locate and update the code.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/writers/duckdb.py`:
- Line 219: The CREATE TABLE call injects unescaped table/column names (e.g.,
using table_name derived from FramePacket.table) leading to SQL injection;
update the code paths that build SQL (the conn.execute call shown and related
functions like _sync_schema and _upsert_data) to escape/quote identifiers
properly before interpolating them: implement a small helper to safely quote
DuckDB identifiers (wrap with double quotes and escape any embedded double
quotes) and use that helper for table and column names everywhere instead of
direct f-string interpolation; continue to use parameterized bindings for data
values where applicable.
- Around line 153-169: close() currently acquires self._lock and then awaits
self.flush(), which can deadlock if flush() later requires the same lock; move
the await self.flush() call to run before acquiring self._lock so flush executes
without holding the lock, then enter async with self._lock only to check/close
self._conn and set self._conn = None (keep the try/except/finally and
self._logger.warning as-is) to ensure connection teardown remains protected.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/src/vertex_forager/writers/memory.py`:
- Around line 37-68: collect_table reads shared state self._tables without
synchronization while write() uses self._lock, so wrap the entire
read/concat/sort sequence in the same lock to prevent concurrent mutation;
specifically acquire self._lock at the start of collect_table (use the context
manager form if _lock supports it, e.g., with self._lock:) before accessing
self._tables and keep the lock held until after df is constructed (and before
return) so parts are consistent; ensure you use the same lock object used by
write() and release it in all paths (try/finally or context manager) rather than
changing collect_table to async.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/tests/conftest.py`:
- Around line 169-174: FramePacket.observed_at is being passed a string; change
it to a datetime object to satisfy Pydantic validation. Locate the
factory/fixture returning FramePacket (the function that constructs FramePacket
in tests/conftest.py) and replace the string "2024-01-02T00:00:00Z" with a
proper datetime instance (e.g., datetime(2024,1,2,0,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc) or
parsed via datetime.fromisoformat/pendulum/dateutil) so observed_at is a
datetime, not a string.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/tests/integration/test_factory.py`:
- Around line 44-50: The test uses patch.dict(os.environ, {}, clear=True) which
wipes all system env vars; change the test to only remove or override the
specific API-related variable(s) instead: use patch.dict(os.environ,
{"API_KEY_NAME": ""}, clear=False) or use monkeypatch.delenv("API_KEY_NAME",
raising=False) so that create_client(provider="sharadar", rate_limit=100) runs
with only the API key absent; update the test around create_client to target the
exact env var name checked by create_client rather than clearing the entire
os.environ.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/tests/integration/test_pagination.py`:
- Line 1: Remove the unnecessary leading blank line(s) at the top of
test_pagination.py so the file begins immediately with the first import or test
definition (e.g., the first import/pytest code), ensuring no empty lines precede
the module content.
- Around line 6-25: The test calls _fetch_pagination which directly invokes
create_writer(connect_db) and create_router(...), so patch both factory
functions in the test (in addition to tqdm and the client's _run) to prevent
real writer/router creation; specifically, patch create_writer and create_router
on vertex_forager.providers.sharadar.client, set their return_value to a mock
async context manager (provide __aenter__ and __aexit__ as AsyncMock) and if
needed set the router mock to return a writer-like object or expected
attributes, then run the same assertions on tqdm.disable via
mock_tqdm.call_args.kwargs.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/tests/integration/test_router_factory.py`:
- Around line 28-32: Tests currently assert private attributes on the
SharadarRouter instance (router._api_key, router._rate_limit,
router._start_date); change the test to verify the same behavior via the public
interface (e.g., call SharadarRouter's public properties or methods such as
router.api_key, router.rate_limit, router.start_date or existing
getters/behavior that depends on those values). If the class lacks suitable
public accessors, add clear, read-only properties or methods on SharadarRouter
to expose the configured API key, rate limit and start date and then update the
test to assert against those public members instead of the underscored
internals.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/tests/unit/test_duckdb_writer.py`:
- Around line 26-53: The test currently instantiates DuckDBWriter directly and
never closes it, which can leave files locked on Windows/CI; change the test to
use the async context manager provided by BaseWriter (e.g., "async with
DuckDBWriter(db_path) as writer:") so the writer is properly awaited and closed
after use, then call await writer.write(packet) inside that block and proceed
with the DuckDB assertions; reference DuckDBWriter and its async context manager
methods (__aenter__/__aexit__ inherited from BaseWriter) to locate where to
update the test.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/tests/unit/test_http_executor.py`:
- Around line 169-199: The test class TestHttpExecutorConcurrency uses the
mock_async_client fixture but that fixture is currently defined inside
TestHttpExecutor and thus not reachable; move the mock_async_client fixture to
module-level (or shared conftest) so TestHttpExecutorConcurrency can access it,
and add the `@pytest.mark.asyncio` decorator to the async
test_executor_handles_concurrent_requests method so pytest runs it as an async
test; keep function names RequestSpec, HttpExecutor, and executor.fetch
unchanged while updating fixture scope and adding the decorator.
- Around line 73-118: The three async test coroutines
test_fetch_successful_request, test_fetch_handles_http_errors, and
test_fetch_handles_network_errors are missing pytest's asyncio marker; add
`@pytest.mark.asyncio` above each async test or add `@pytest.mark.asyncio` to the
TestHttpExecutor test class so pytest runs them as async tests, ensuring the
async event loop is available when invoking HttpExecutor.fetch and when using
the AsyncMock mock_async_client.

In `@packages/vertex-forager/tests/unit/test_pipeline.py`:
- Around line 26-28: The test uses AsyncMock mock_writer with a synchronous
lambda as side_effect; change this so write returns awaitable: either set
mock_writer.write.return_value to an appropriate WriteResult or replace the

marker at the TestSharadarRouterUnit class level) so router.generate_jobs
coroutine tests are executed correctly.
- Around line 299-314: TestRouterEdgeCases uses a non-existent fixture
sharadar_router; either rename the parameter to the existing router fixture or
add a sharadar_router fixture that returns the same SharadarRouter instance.

and test_router_maintains_api_rate_limits) to accept router instead of
sharadar_router, or implement a sharadar_router fixture that constructs/returns
the SharadarRouter used elsewhere (ensuring it exposes _rate_limit and
```
